name: Distill long emails with MySQL and Twilio for your team

options.systemMessage: You are a conversational ai specialist for the ai agents team. Use the attached tools to look up real data before answering — never invent request details. Keep answers under 150 words, cite which tool you used, and escalate anything you cannot verify.

parameters.toolDescription: Fetch live request data from the internal REST API by id or query.

parameters.message: =✅ Distill long emails with MySQL and Twilio for your team — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf00985-request`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Normalize Request Payload

### Normalize Request Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming request records to a stable schema

Next: Reason & Act (AI Agent)

### Reason & Act (AI Agent)

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Ports: ①→ Shape Agent Output · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Think It Through

Scratchpad tool: lets the agent reason step-by-step before acting.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `tool`

### Lookup API Data

Tool the agent can call to hit the REST API with parameters it chooses at runtime.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `tool`

### Run Calculations

Math tool — keeps arithmetic exact instead of letting the LLM guess.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `tool`

### Shape Agent Output

Shapes the item: writes `answer`, `confidence`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Confident Enough?

### Confident Enough?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.confidence }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Deliver Answer + Log to MySQL · ②→ Escalate to Human Queue

### Deliver Answer + Log to MySQL

mySql — `insert` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Join Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

nodes.name: Note: Deliver Answer + Log to MySQL

parameters.content:
### Escalate to Human Queue

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Join Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Join Outcomes

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

notion — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Load Previous Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Load Previous Snapshot

googleSheets — `read` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per request (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

### Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

Delegates to a reusable sub-workflow (single-responsibility building block) and waits for its result.

Ports: ①→ Record Sub-workflow Result · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Record Sub-workflow Result

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Twilio · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Twilio

twilio — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

notion — `create` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

# Distill long emails with MySQL and Twilio for your team

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Architect · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Distill long emails with MySQL and Twilio for your team. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual conversational ai is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Normalize Request Payload → Reason & Act (AI Agent) → Shape Agent Output → Confident Enough? → Deliver Answer + Log to MySQL → Join Outcomes → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating conversational ai in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Improves response time
- Scales without extra headcount
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Groq API key; MySQL connection; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your conversational ai process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
